title: From Clay Tablets to Electric Pages: How Printing Transformed the World
title_zh: 从泥板到电子书：印刷术如何改变世界
intro_zh: 本文约1000词，介绍印刷术从诞生到现代的发展历程及其对知识传播、社会变革和文化交流的深远影响。文章包含具体历史事例和数据，适合CET-4水平阅读。

paragraphs.en: Before printing, knowledge was a precious treasure locked away in handwritten manuscripts. Monks in Europe spent years copying a single Bible, while in China, scholars meticulously transcribed classic texts by hand. This process was so slow and expensive that books were owned only by the rich and powerful. The majority of people could not read or write, and information traveled at the speed of a horse or a ship. Then came a revolutionary invention that would change everything.
paragraphs.zh: 在印刷术出现之前，知识是一件珍贵的珍宝，被锁在手抄本中。欧洲的僧侣要花数年才能抄完一本《圣经》，而中国的学者则细致地手抄经典文本。这个过程非常缓慢且昂贵，书籍只有富人和权贵才能拥有。大多数人不会读写，信息传播的速度与马匹或船只相当。随后，一项革命性的发明改变了这一切。

paragraphs.en: The first printing revolution began in China. Around 1040 AD, a Chinese inventor named Bi Sheng created movable type using clay. Each character was a separate piece that could be arranged, inked, and pressed onto paper. This method was much faster than hand-copying. However, because Chinese has thousands of characters, the system was complex. For centuries, its use was limited. Meanwhile, in Korea, metal movable type was developed, but its impact remained regional.
paragraphs.zh: 第一次印刷革命始于中国。公元1040年左右，中国发明家毕昇用黏土制作了活字。每个字都是一块独立的字模，可以排列、上墨并压印到纸上。这种方法比手抄快得多。然而，由于中文有数千个字，该系统很复杂。几个世纪以来，它的使用是有限的。与此同时，朝鲜发展了金属活字，但其影响仍局限于地区。

paragraphs.en: The real breakthrough came in Europe in the 1450s. A German goldsmith named Johannes Gutenberg invented a printing press that used movable metal type, oil-based ink, and a wine-press mechanism. His most famous work, the Gutenberg Bible, was printed around 1455. By 1500, an estimated 20 million books had been printed across Europe—more than all books produced in the previous 1,000 years. This explosion of books made knowledge accessible to many more people.
paragraphs.zh: 真正的突破发生在15世纪50年代的欧洲。德国金匠约翰内斯·古腾堡发明了一种使用金属活字、油基墨水和压酒机机制的印刷机。他最著名的作品《古腾堡圣经》大约在1455年印刷。到1500年，整个欧洲估计印刷了2000万本书——超过此前1000年里生产的所有书籍。书籍的激增使得更多人能够获取知识。

paragraphs.en: Printing transformed education. Before, students had to rely on teachers reading from rare manuscripts. After printing, identical textbooks could be produced cheaply. This allowed schools and universities to expand rapidly. For example, by 1500, most European cities had printing shops, and literacy rates began to rise. Ideas such as Martin Luther's Reformation spread quickly because pamphlets could be printed in large quantities. The printing press helped break the monopoly of the church and rulers on information.
paragraphs.zh: 印刷术改变了教育。在此之前，学生必须依赖教师朗读稀有的手稿。之后，相同的课本可以廉价生产。这使得中小学和大学得以迅速扩展。例如，到1500年，大多数欧洲城市都有印刷作坊，识字率开始上升。像马丁·路德的宗教改革这样的思想传播得很快，因为小册子可以大量印刷。印刷机帮助打破了教会和统治者对信息的垄断。

paragraphs.en: Science also benefited enormously. Before printing, scientific knowledge was often limited to personal notes and oral tradition. With printed books, researchers could share their findings widely. The famous astronomer Copernicus published his revolutionary theory in a printed book in 1543. Soon after, Galileo's works were distributed across Europe. Printing allowed for accurate diagrams and data tables, which reduced errors in copying. This accelerated the pace of scientific discovery.
paragraphs.zh: 科学也受益匪浅。印刷术出现前，科学知识往往局限于个人笔记和口述传统。有了印刷书籍，研究者可以广泛分享他们的发现。著名天文学家哥白尼在1543年出版了他的革命性理论。不久后，伽利略的著作传遍欧洲。印刷术使得准确的图表和数据表格成为可能，减少了手抄中的错误。这加速了科学发现的步伐。

paragraphs.en: The impact of printing was not just intellectual; it changed daily life. Printed maps helped explorers like Columbus navigate new routes. Printed music allowed people to sing and play together even if they had never met. In the 18th century, newspapers and magazines emerged, creating a new public sphere where ideas could be debated. The printing press also gave rise to modern advertising, as businesses used posters and flyers to attract customers. In short, printing became the engine of commerce and culture.
paragraphs.zh: 印刷术的影响不仅仅是智力上的；它改变了日常生活。印刷地图帮助像哥伦布这样的探险家开辟新航线。印刷乐谱使人们即使从未谋面也能一起唱歌演奏。18世纪，报纸和杂志出现，创造了一个可以辩论思想的新的公共领域。印刷机还催生了现代广告，因为商家使用海报和传单吸引顾客。简而言之，印刷术成为了商业和文化的引擎。

paragraphs.en: Today, we live in the digital age, where information is available at the click of a mouse. But the principal idea remains the same: making knowledge available to all. From books to newspapers to the internet, printing laid the foundation for mass communication. It empowered the common person, encouraged critical thinking, and built a more open society. The next time you read a book or scroll through a webpage, remember that it all started with a simple device that pressed ink onto paper.
paragraphs.zh: 今天，我们生活在数字时代，信息只需点击鼠标即可获得。但主要理念依然不变：让所有人都能获得知识。从书籍到报纸再到互联网，印刷术为大众传播奠定了基础。它赋予普通人力量，鼓励批判性思维，并构建了一个更开放的社会。下次当你阅读一本书或滚动浏览网页时，请记住，这一切都始于一个将墨水压到纸上的简单装置。

paragraphs.en: Looking back, printing was not just a technological invention; it was a social revolution. It democratized knowledge, sparked the Renaissance, the Reformation, and the Scientific Revolution. Without it, the modern world as we know it would not exist. As we embrace new digital tools, we should appreciate and preserve the transformative power of the printed word.
paragraphs.zh: 回顾过去，印刷术不仅仅是一项技术发明；它是一场社会革命。它使知识民主化，引发了文艺复兴、宗教改革和科学革命。没有它，我们所知的现代世界将不复存在。当我们拥抱新的数字工具时，我们应该感激并保护印刷文字带来的变革力量。

questions.q: What is the main idea of this passage?
options: Printing is the most important invention in history.
Printing transformed knowledge distribution and society.
Bi Sheng invented printing before Gutenberg.
Digital technology has replaced printing today.
questions.explain: 主旨题。文章主要讲述印刷术如何改变知识传播和社会，选项B最全面。选项A太绝对，C只是细节，D不符合文章结尾的论述。

questions.q: According to the passage, what was a major effect of the printing press in Europe around 1500?
options: The number of printed books decreased after 1500.
Literacy rates began to rise as books became cheaper.
Only the rich could afford printed books.
options_en: The number of printed books decreased after 1500.
Literacy rates began to rise as books became cheaper.
Only the rich could afford printed books.
questions.explain: 细节题。第四段提到印刷使得课本便宜，学校扩展，识字率上升。选项B正确。A与事实相反，C与文章相反，D未提及。

questions.q: What can be inferred about scientific knowledge before printing?
options: It was widely shared through oral tradition.
It was more accurate than after printing.
It was mainly preserved in handwritten notes and oral communication.
It was published in newspapers and journals.
questions.explain: 推断题。第五段提到科学知识限于个人笔记和口述传统，因此可推知主要保存在手写笔记和口头交流中。选项C正确。A虽提到口述，但未提广泛共享；B错误，印刷后更准确；D不符合时间顺序。

questions.q: The word 'explosion' in paragraph 3 is closest in meaning to ________.
questions.explain: 词义题。'explosion' 在文中指书籍数量激增，所以选B“突然增加”。A是字面意思，C是破坏性事件，D与文意相反。
